title: Examples
text: two phylogenetic trees agrobacteria isolates plotted widget, one multilocus sequence analysis (MLSA) another time-calibrated BEAST tree, following study: Alexandra J. Weisberg et al., Unexpected conservation global transmission agrobacterial virulence plasmids. Science 368, eaba5256 (2020)
code: library(heattree)  data(weisberg_2020_metadata) data(weisberg_2020_mlsa) data(weisberg_2020_beast)  heat_tree(   tree = list('MLSA' = weisberg_2020_mlsa, 'BEAST' = weisberg_2020_beast),   metadata = list(weisberg_2020_metadata, weisberg_2020_metadata),   aesthetics = list(     c(tipLabelText = 'strain', tipLabelColor = 'host_type'),     c(tipLabelText = 'strain', tipLabelColor = 'year_isolated')   ),   manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE )

title: Examples
text: Phylogenetic trees comparative genomics study Xylella: Bansal, K., Kumar, S., Kaur, ., Singh, ., & Patil, P. B. (2021). Deep phylo-taxono genomics reveals Xylella variant lineage plant associated Xanthomonas supports taxonomic reunification along Stenotrophomonas Pseudoxanthomonas. Genomics, 113(6), 3989-4003.
code: library(heattree)  data(bansal_2021_metadata) data(bansal_2021_tree)  heat_tree(   tree = bansal_2021_tree,   metadata = bansal_2021_metadata,   aesthetics = c(tipLabelColor = 'Lifestyle'),   manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE )

title: Examples
text: phytools package many example trees associated metadata, ideal use heattree. data sets single heattree widget.
code: # Load required datasets from phytools library(phytools) data(anole.data) data(anoletree) data(bonyfish.data) data(bonyfish.tree) data(butterfly.data) data(butterfly.tree) data(eel.data) data(eel.tree) data(flatworm.data) data(flatworm.tree) data(mammal.data) data(mammal.tree) data(primate.data) data(primate.tree) data(sunfish.data) data(sunfish.tree) data(tropidurid.data) data(tropidurid.tree)  # Create tree widget library(heattree) heat_tree(   tree = list(     'Anoles' = anoletree,     'Bony fishs' = bonyfish.tree,     'Butterflies' = butterfly.tree,     'Eels' = eel.tree,     'Flatworms' = flatworm.tree,     'Mammals' = mammal.tree,     'Primates' = primate.tree,     'Sunfishes' = sunfish.tree,     'Tortoises' = tropidurid.tree   ),   metadata = list(     anole.data,     bonyfish.data,     butterfly.data,     eel.data,     flatworm.data,     mammal.data,     primate.data,     sunfish.data,     tropidurid.data   ),   aesthetics = list(     c(tipLabelColor = 'SVL'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'paternal_care'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'habitat'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'feed_mode'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'Habitat'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'bodyMass'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'Skull_length'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'buccal.length'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'body_height')   ),   layout = 'circular', manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE )

title: Getting started
text: package designed simple use possible also allowing advanced customization. fact, since can upload tree metadata interactively, entirely valid create widget input: can also supply trees/metadata plot widget created. following types input data currently supported: Paths Newick Nexus files Newick-formatted text (character vector single item) phylo objects ape package example, test data included package three formats: three commands produce plot: can also supply metadata tree supplying path TSV/CSV data.frame/tibble. table must column values matching IDs tree file. heattree automatically identify column IDs, can name. example, data associated example tree: Similar tree input, paths parsed data accepted. Metadata can used color size tree elements, similar ggplot2 works. aesthetics parameter used specify columns correspond aesthetics. two commands produce plot: Check heat-tree JavaScript package documentation list valid aesthetics.
code: library(heattree) heat_tree() weisberg_2020_mlsa_path <- system.file('extdata', 'weisberg_2020_mlsa.tre', package = 'heattree') print(weisberg_2020_mlsa_path) #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpDx2vts/temp_libpath252546918c6c/heattree/extdata/weisberg_2020_mlsa.tre"  weisberg_2020_mlsa_text <- readLines(weisberg_2020_mlsa_path) print(substr(weisberg_2020_mlsa_text, 1, 100)) #> [1] "(((((CG678__BV3:1.0000005e-06,CG78__BV3:1.0000005e-06)92.0:0.009478810652,((T268_95__BV3:1.0000005e-"  print(weisberg_2020_mlsa)  # The already parsed version in the ape phylo format #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 86 tips and 36 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   CG678__BV3, CG78__BV3, T268_95__BV3, BM37_95__BV3, F2_5__BV3, AV25_95__BV3, ... #> Node labels: #>   100.0, 100.0, 100.0, 87.0, 92.0, 76.0, ... #>  #> Rooted; includes branch length(s). heat_tree(weisberg_2020_mlsa_path, manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE) heat_tree(weisberg_2020_mlsa_text, manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE) heat_tree(weisberg_2020_mlsa, manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE) Note: `manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE` is used in these examples since the visualizations are part of a scrollable document and the zoom interfers with scrolling. By default, plots can be zoomed and panned. Remove `manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE` to try it out. weisberg_2020_metadata_path <- system.file('extdata', 'weisberg_2020_metadata.tsv', package = 'heattree') weisberg_2020_metadata <- readr::read_tsv(weisberg_2020_metadata_path) print(weisberg_2020_metadata) #> # A tibble: 148 × 13 #>    strain   species genus genomospecies host  host_type opine_type year_isolated #>    <chr>    <chr>   <chr> <chr>         <chr> <chr>     <chr>              <dbl> #>  1 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Rose  Woody     Agrocinop…          1979 #>  2 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Grape Grape (W… NA                  1995 #>  3 Agrobac… Agroba… Agro… Agrobacteriu… Euon… Woody     Agrocinop…          1973 #>  4 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Rose  Woody     Nopaline              NA #>  5 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Cher… Woody     Nopaline            2008 #>  6 Agrobac… Agroba… Agro… Biovar 1      Ston… Herbaceo… Nopaline            1958 #>  7 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Grape Grape (W… Nopaline            1995 #>  8 Allorhi… Allorh… Allo… Biovar 3      Grape Grape (W… Vitopine/…          1995 #>  9 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Olal… Woody     Nopaline            1993 #> 10 Rhizobi… Rhizob… Rhiz… Biovar 2      Peach Woody     Nopaline            2009 #> # ℹ 138 more rows #> # ℹ 5 more variables: plasmid_class <chr>, plasmid_type <chr>, #> #   ncbi_accession <chr>, mlsa_node_id <chr>, beast_node_id <chr> heat_tree(   tree = weisberg_2020_mlsa,   metadata = weisberg_2020_metadata,   aesthetics = c(tipLabelText = 'strain', tipLabelColor = 'host_type'),   manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE )

title: Getting started
text: Although widget primarily designed interactive use, initial settings can set programmatically. value options described heat-tree JavaScript package documentation can used optional parameters. example, layout can changed circular like :
code: heat_tree(   tree = weisberg_2020_mlsa,   metadata = weisberg_2020_metadata,   aesthetics = c(tipLabelText = 'strain', tipLabelColor = 'host_type'),   manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE, layout = 'circular' )

title: Getting started
text: heat_tree widget capable managing multiple trees . initialize widget multiple trees, supply lists tree metadata inputs. metadata/aesthetics also supplied must list well corresponds list trees. example:
code: heat_tree(   tree = list(     'Weisberg 2020 MLSA' = weisberg_2020_mlsa,     'Weisberg 2020 Beast' = weisberg_2020_beast,     'Bansal 2021' = bansal_2021_tree   ),   metadata = list(weisberg_2020_metadata, weisberg_2020_metadata, bansal_2021_metadata),   aesthetics = list(     c(tipLabelText = 'strain', tipLabelColor = 'host_type'),     c(tipLabelText = 'strain', tipLabelColor = 'year_isolated'),     c(tipLabelColor = 'Lifestyle')   ),   manualZoomAndPanEnabled = FALSE )

title: Getting started
text: documentation covers aspects creating tree visualizations R. information extensive controls embedded widget, refer documentation JavaScript package implements .

what: A Self-Contained Widget for Interactive Phylogenetic Tree Visualization
title: A Self-Contained Widget for Interactive Phylogenetic Tree Visualization
text: package makes easy visualize, manipulate, export phylogenetic trees R using interactive viewer/editor. can insert tree viewer Rmd/Quarto documents make trees accessible online. want use web development outside R, consider javascript heat-tree package package builds upon.

title: A Self-Contained Widget for Interactive Phylogenetic Tree Visualization
text: now, heattree available Github can installed devtools:

title: A Self-Contained Widget for Interactive Phylogenetic Tree Visualization
text: package includes example data sets automatically loaded package (e.g. weisberg_2020_mlsa), can try minimal effort. installing packages, simply run lines get idea works:  See “Getting Started” page interactive examples
code: library(heattree) heat_tree(   tree = weisberg_2020_mlsa,   metadata = weisberg_2020_metadata,   aesthetics = c(tipLabelColor = 'host_type'),   layout = 'circular')

title: A Self-Contained Widget for Interactive Phylogenetic Tree Visualization

title: Shiny bindings for heat_tree — heat_tree-shiny
text: Output render functions using heat_tree within Shiny applications interactive Rmd documents.

title: Shiny bindings for heat_tree — heat_tree-shiny
code: heat_treeOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  renderheat_tree(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for heat_tree — heat_tree-shiny
text: outputId output variable read width, height Must valid CSS unit (like '100%', '400px', 'auto') number, coerced string 'px' appended. expr expression generates heat_tree env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr quoted expression (quote())? useful want save expression variable.

what: Create an interactive phylogenetic tree — heat_tree
title: Create an interactive phylogenetic tree — heat_tree
text: Create interactive phylogenetic tree using javascript heat-tree package

title: Create an interactive phylogenetic tree — heat_tree
code: heat_tree(   tree = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   aesthetics = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   elementId = NULL,   ... )

title: Create an interactive phylogenetic tree — heat_tree
text: tree One trees plot. Can raw newick-formatted string, phylo object, list inputs. list provided, names used name trees. metadata Metadata associated tree. Can data.frame tibble column containing IDs matching tree labels. ID column detected automatically. multiple trees (list), list tables equal length required. list provided, names used name metadata tables. aesthetics named character vector defining metadata columns initially used color/size tree parts. multiple trees (list), list equal length required. width Width widget (CSS units number). height Height widget (CSS units number). elementId Optional element ID widget. ... Options passed options parameter underlying HeatTree.heatTree() javascript function modify initial state widget.

title: Create an interactive phylogenetic tree — heat_tree
code:
# Create an empty tree viewer for loading data interactively heat_tree()  {"x":{"trees":[],"options":[]},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]} # Create a tree viewer with example data included with the package data(weisberg_2020_metadata) data(weisberg_2020_mlsa) heat_tree(   tree = weisberg_2020_mlsa,   metadata = weisberg_2020_metadata,   aesthetics = c(tipLabelColor = 'host_type') )
